event: The Departure of Saint Ursula from Basle
symbols: ship departing — the journey of faith, the Church as a vessel carrying souls
crowded vessel of companions — the community of the faithful on pilgrimage
harbor city — the departure from safety into the unknown, trusting in God
description: A Gothic arched panel showing a busy harbor scene. A large sailing vessel dominates the center, packed with numerous figures — women in white headscarves and men in varied dress. Saint Ursula stands or sits prominently among them. The ship has a tall mast with furled sails and rigging. In the background, a medieval city with towers, walls, and churches rises along the waterfront. Additional figures crowd the dock and a smaller boat in the foreground. The painting is richly detailed with the meticulous precision of early Netherlandish art. The water is calm and green-blue.

_meta.title: The Departure of Saint Ursula from Basle, panel from The Reliquary of St. Ursula
